chapter: Chapter 2: The Invisible Living World: Beyond Our Naked Eye

questions.question: Why are microscopes essential for studying microorganisms?
questions.explanation: Microscopes magnify tiny organisms that cannot be seen with the naked eye.

questions.question: Robert Hooke discovered cells by observing which material under a microscope?
questions.explanation: Hooke observed cork and discovered small compartments called cells.

questions.question: Which scientist first observed living microorganisms?
questions.explanation: Leeuwenhoek observed microorganisms using improved microscopes.

questions.question: Which cell component regulates the movement of substances into and out of the cell?
questions.explanation: The cell membrane controls entry and exit of substances.

questions.question: Which structure is responsible for controlling cell activities?
questions.explanation: The nucleus controls all activities of the cell.

questions.question: Which structure is found only in plant cells?
questions.explanation: Plant cells have a cell wall that provides support.

questions.question: What is the main role of cytoplasm in the cell?
questions.explanation: Cytoplasm is the site where most metabolic activities occur.

questions.question: Which microorganisms convert milk into curd?
questions.explanation: Lactobacillus bacteria convert milk sugar into lactic acid.

questions.question: Which process is responsible for dough rising during bread making?
questions.explanation: Yeast fermentation produces carbon dioxide which causes dough to rise.

questions.question: Which microorganisms fix nitrogen in the soil?
questions.explanation: Rhizobium bacteria fix atmospheric nitrogen in soil.

questions.question: Which microorganism belongs to the protozoa group?
questions.explanation: Amoeba is a protozoan microorganism.

questions.question: Which organisms produce oxygen in aquatic ecosystems?
questions.explanation: Microalgae perform photosynthesis and produce oxygen.

questions.question: Which level of organization comes directly after cells?
questions.explanation: Cells combine to form tissues.

questions.question: Which microorganisms are mainly responsible for decomposition?
questions.explanation: Decomposer microbes break down organic matter.

questions.question: Which type of organism consists of only one cell?
questions.explanation: Unicellular organisms consist of a single cell.

questions.question: Which part of the plant cell performs photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Chloroplasts contain chlorophyll and perform photosynthesis.

questions.question: Which process converts organic waste into compost?
questions.explanation: Decomposition breaks down organic matter into compost.

questions.question: Which gas is produced during yeast fermentation?
questions.explanation: Yeast releases carbon dioxide during fermentation.

questions.question: Which cell component contains genetic information?
questions.explanation: The nucleus stores genetic material.

questions.question: Which microorganism is commonly found in pond water?
questions.explanation: Amoeba is commonly found in pond water.

questions.question: What is the main function of Rhizobium bacteria?
questions.explanation: Rhizobium bacteria convert atmospheric nitrogen into usable forms for plants.

questions.question: Which organisms are essential for recycling nutrients in ecosystems?
questions.explanation: Decomposer microbes recycle nutrients by breaking down organic matter.

questions.question: Which of the following is a beneficial use of microorganisms?
questions.explanation: Microorganisms are used in food preparation such as bread and curd.

questions.question: Which structure gives rigidity to plant cells?
questions.explanation: The cell wall provides rigidity and support to plant cells.

questions.question: Why are microorganisms important in ecosystems?

options.B: They recycle nutrients and support life

questions.explanation: Microorganisms recycle nutrients and maintain ecological balance.
